segments.text: To the choirmaster. Of David, the servant of the LORD.
Transgression speaks to the wicked deep in his heart; there is no fear of God before his eyes.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For he flatters himself in his own eyes
that his iniquity cannot be found out and hated.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: until…: Heb. to find his iniquity to hate

segments.text: The words of his mouth are trouble and deceit; he has ceased to act wisely and do good.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He plots trouble while on his bed; he sets himself in a way that is not good; he does not reject evil.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Your steadfast love, O LORD, extends to the heavens, your faithfulness to the clouds.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Your righteousness is like the mountains of God; your judgments are like the great deep; man and beast you save, O LORD.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: the…: Heb. the mountains of God

segments.text: How precious is your steadfast love, O God! The children of mankind take refuge in the shadow of your wings.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: They are abundantly satisfied with the fatness of your house, and you give them drink from the river of your delights.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For with you is the fountain of life; in your light do we see light.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Oh, continue your steadfast love to those who know you, and your righteousness to the upright of heart!
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: continue: Heb. draw out at length

segments.text: Let not the foot of arrogance come upon me, nor the hand of the wicked drive me away.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: There the evildoers lie fallen; they are thrust down, unable to rise.
segments.speaker: David
